january.alert: Winter months — rats actively move indoors for warmth. Check roof spaces and wall gaps. Bed bugs remain active year-round indoors.
january.tip: Seal gaps around pipes and doors before the cool season ends. Check stored winter clothing for bed bugs.

february.alert: Termite swarming begins in the south. Bee scout activity increases as spring approaches. Continue rat monitoring.
february.tip: Inspect wooden structures for termite mud tubes. Contact a beekeeper if you see scout bees investigating wall cavities.

march.alert: Scorpion season begins in Rajasthan and Gujarat. Wasp queens emerge to build new nests. First mosquito breeding cycles start.
march.tip: Shake shoes before wearing. Clear rockpiles and woodpiles around the home. Drain any standing water.

april.alert: Scorpion and snake activity surges with heat. Wasp nests rapidly expanding. Mosquito population accelerating.
april.tip: Check under stones and in shoes daily in high-risk states. Install door sweeps. Empty water containers twice weekly.

may.alert: Peak heat drives scorpions, snakes, and centipedes indoors. Dengue vector mosquitoes (Aedes) become active.
may.tip: Use UV blacklights at night to detect scorpions. Seal all ground-level gaps. Apply DEET before going outdoors.

june.alert: Monsoon onset: mosquito populations explode. Termites swarm. Frogs appear near homes (do not harm). Bat activity peaks.
june.tip: CRITICAL: Empty ALL water containers every 3 days. Frogs found near/in home are protected — guide them outside gently.

july.alert: Peak monsoon: highest disease transmission period. Dengue, malaria, leptospirosis risk at maximum. Cockroach population surges.
july.tip: Wear full-sleeved clothing outdoors. Use mosquito nets. Never walk barefoot in flooded areas (leptospirosis from rat urine).

august.alert: Continued monsoon pest pressure. Housefly populations at annual maximum due to wet organic matter. Bed bugs unaffected by monsoon.
august.tip: Keep garbage in sealed bins. Cover all food. Check window screens for integrity. Empty AC water trays daily.

september.alert: Post-monsoon: high standing water after rains creates extended mosquito breeding. Scorpions and snakes still active.
september.tip: Check for new standing water after rain events. Continue dengue prevention vigilance. Inspect compound walls for scorpions.

october.alert: Cool season begins: mosquito activity reducing but dengue still transmitting. Rats begin moving indoors for winter. Termites complete swarming.
october.tip: Seal gaps for winter rat-proofing now. Continue mosquito prevention through October. Inspect for termite damage after monsoon.

november.alert: Winter transition: mosquito activity declining significantly. Rats, cockroaches, and bed bugs remain active year-round indoors.
november.tip: Rat-proof the home before winter: check all gaps, store food in sealed containers, install snap traps as monitoring tools.

december.alert: Winter indoor pest season: rats, cockroaches, and bed bugs are the primary concerns. Scorpions and snakes dormant in most of India.
december.tip: Check stored clothing and bedding for bed bugs. Maintain kitchen hygiene to deter cockroaches. Monitor rat traps.

rajasthan.note: India's highest scorpion density. Indian Red Scorpion (Hottentotta tamulus) present. Anti-venom available at major government hospitals.

kerala.note: Nipah virus hotspot (bat-borne). Highest snake diversity in India. Peak monsoon disease transmission.

maharashtra.note: Major scorpion zone (Konkan belt). Urban Mumbai: high rat and cockroach pressure. Dengue outbreaks annual.

west_bengal.note: Japanese encephalitis zone in rural areas. High Leptospirosis risk during floods. Urban Kolkata: stray dog and rat pressure.

karnataka.note: Kyasanur Forest Disease (tick-borne) in Malnad region. High termite pressure in wooden structures. Spectacled Cobra common.

gujarat.note: CCHF (tick-borne) documented in livestock farmers. High scorpion density in rocky zones. Malaria in tribal districts.
